role: Member of Technical Staff (Software Engineer, Agent Capabilities)
hero_headline: Agent capabilities are only as good as the tools you give the model. I build those tools in production, every day. This page went up 12 minutes after the role posted.
hero_subline: Comet's agents need real capabilities: typed tools, permission gates, and evals that catch a bad action before it ships. That is exactly the layer I work in. My agents call real functions over MCP, not free-text guesses, and they run unsupervised 24/7.
pitch_oneliner: Week 1: take one capability that Comet still does in free-text, give it a typed tool contract with a permission gate and a regression eval, and ship it so the agent completes the action reliably instead of improvising it.

jd_match.requirement: Build and expand the capabilities (tools, actions) available to AI agents
jd_match.proof: empresaAI: 7 agents that call typed tools to read email, build a Next.js app, deploy to Vercel and publish, over a file-based bus with deterministic state.

jd_match.requirement: Design tool interfaces and context engineering for frontier models
jd_match.proof: I build tools on the Claude API and MCP so agents invoke real functions with typed contracts and permission gates, not unconstrained text.

jd_match.requirement: Evals and guardrails so agents act correctly, not just plausibly
jd_match.proof: ManoloBot runs an automated QA gate (Playwright, 3 viewports, JS-syntax checks) on every app before it goes live; nothing ships unverified.
Python and TypeScript daily across empresaAI and ManoloBot; full-stack on Next.js, Vercel and Supabase end to end.
